description: Small bundled research-only text retrieval set for reproducible vector-search evaluation.

docs.text: Astronomers use telescopes to observe planets, stars, moons, and distant galaxies during clear night skies.
Orbital mechanics explains how gravity keeps planets moving around the sun on predictable paths.
Bakers mix flour, water, yeast, and salt so dough can rise before bread is baked in a hot oven.
Cooks boil pasta in salted water and finish it with sauce, herbs, and grated cheese.
Marine biologists study whales, dolphins, and seals in cold ocean waters and coastal habitats.
Coral reefs support fish, sea turtles, and colorful invertebrates in warm tropical seas.
Roman engineers built stone roads, bridges, and aqueducts across their empire to move armies and goods.
Ancient Egyptian builders raised pyramids and temples beside the Nile using stone blocks and organized labor.
Python programs use functions, modules, and readable syntax for automation, scripting, and data analysis.
SQL databases store tables, enforce schemas, and answer queries with joins, filters, and indexes.
Tropical rainforests have heavy rainfall, dense tree canopies, and rich biodiversity across many species.
Deserts receive very little rain, swing between hot days and cold nights, and support drought-tolerant plants.

queries.text: Which passage is about using telescopes to observe planets and stars?
Which document explains how gravity keeps planets moving around the sun?
Which text talks about yeast, dough rising, and baking bread in an oven?
Which passage is about whales and dolphins living in ocean habitats?
Which document is about Roman engineers building roads, bridges, and aqueducts?
Which text is about Python functions, modules, and readable syntax for automation?
Which passage describes heavy rainfall and biodiversity in tropical rainforests?
